# Pizza Dough Calculator - Repo-Specific Rules

This repo is a small static frontend app with domain-sensitive dough and schedule logic.

## Source of truth

- Current shipped behavior lives in `docs/spec/current-product-spec.md`.
- Formula and model details live in `docs/spec/formulas-and-data.md`.
- Code navigation notes live in `docs/code-map.md`.
- Manual verification expectations live in `docs/testing/manual-test-checklist.md`.

Do not treat `AGENTS.md` or `CLAUDE.md` as the source of truth for product formulas or shipped behavior.

## Non-negotiable constraints

- Keep the project zero-dependency unless the user explicitly approves a different architecture.
- Do not introduce npm, frameworks, bundlers, TypeScript, a backend, or external services without explicit approval.
- Preserve the static-hosting model.
- Prefer small, direct changes over abstractions added just in case.

## Code and domain guardrails

- `state` in `script.js` is the single source of truth.
- Calculation paths must not read from the DOM.
- Internal weight state stays metric and internal temperature state stays Celsius.
- `state.leavener` must remain one of `'idy'`, `'fresh'`, or `'sourdough'`.
- Sourdough mode must back starter flour and water out of bowl-added flour and water so target ratios stay correct.
- Auto warm-up values should stay on the 15-minute grid unless intentionally redesigned.
- Preserve preset/custom override behavior, unit-toggle display-boundary conversion, and parity between `index.html` and `how-it-works.html` where intended.

## Docs and verification

- If shipped behavior changes, update `docs/spec/current-product-spec.md`.
- If formulas, thresholds, or calibrated constants change, update `docs/spec/formulas-and-data.md`.
- If a user-visible feature ships, update `docs/changelog.md`.
- If future scope changes, update `docs/roadmap/backlog.md`.

After meaningful UI or logic changes, run the relevant parts of `docs/testing/manual-test-checklist.md`. At minimum, verify leavener modes, preset/custom overrides, unit toggle behavior, schedule output, warm-up behavior, mobile layout, and `how-it-works.html` parity when shared UI changes.
